rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Assign owner/due date; close and verify; escalate overdue critical actions.

rules.Question: Is corrective action effectiveness verified after pest activity or treatment?
rules.Requirement Logic: Corrective action must be verified by reduced activity, eliminated source and no recurrence.
rules.Corrective Action: Perform follow-up check; review trends; reopen if recurrence occurs.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No effectiveness verification weakens CAPA and trend engine.
rules.Question_AR: Is corrective action effectiveness verified after pest activity or treatment?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Perform follow-up check; review trends; reopen if recurrence occurs.

rules.Question: Is product, packaging or equipment exposure assessed after pest evidence in food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Pest evidence in food areas requires product impact assessment and disposition.
rules.Corrective Action: Hold/inspect/clean/trace affected products and packaging.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Pest evidence in food area → M08 traceability/HACCP trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M08 traceability; M06 operational; M03 cleaning
rules.Question_AR: Is product, packaging or equipment exposure assessed after pest evidence in food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold/inspect/clean/trace affected products and packaging.

rules.Question: Is cleaning/disinfection performed after pest evidence where contamination may have occurred?
rules.Requirement Logic: Pest evidence requires cleaning/disinfection of affected surfaces/areas and verification before release.
rules.Corrective Action: Clean/disinfect; verify; release area; assess product exposure.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Pest evidence + no cleaning release → M03/M08 trigger.
rules.Question_AR: Is cleaning/disinfection performed after pest evidence where contamination may have occurred?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Clean/disinfect; verify; release area; assess product exposure.

rules.Question: Is there an emergency escalation procedure for infestation or high-risk pest incident?
rules.Requirement Logic: Infestation/high-risk incidents require immediate escalation to QA/management and product hold decisions.
rules.Corrective Action: Stop affected area; escalate; hold product; call contractor; investigate root cause.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Infestation = GMP critical fail and HACCP/M08 trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: Master Engine; M08 traceability; M06 operational
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجدn emergency escalation procedure for infestation or high-risk pest incident?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Stop affected area; escalate; hold product; call contractor; investigate root cause.

rules.Question: If outsourced, is there a contract/SLA defining scope, frequency, target pests, response time, reports and responsibilities?
rules.Requirement Logic: Contractor scope must be defined and aligned with facility risk assessment.
rules.Corrective Action: Define SLA; align service plan with risk assessment.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Poor SLA can lead to monitoring gaps and late response.
rules.Notes: Contractor does not replace FBO responsibility.
rules.Question_AR: If outsourced, is there a contract/SLA defining scope, frequency, target pests, response time, reports and responsibilities?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define SLA; align service plan with risk assessment.

rules.Question: Is the contractor competent/licensed/qualified for food premises pest control?
rules.Requirement Logic: Pest contractor must be competent and use safe, appropriate methods for food environments.
rules.Corrective Action: Verify qualifications; approve contractor; maintain records.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Unqualified contractor applying chemicals may trigger chemical risk review.
rules.Question_AR: هل contractor competent/licensed/qualified for food premises pest control?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Verify qualifications; approve contractor; maintain records.

rules.Question: Do contractor reports include device status, activity, trend observations, chemicals used, recommendations and corrective actions?
rules.Requirement Logic: Service reports must provide actionable data, not generic treatment statements.
rules.Corrective Action: Require structured reports; reject incomplete reports.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Incomplete reports weaken trend and CAPA engine.
rules.Question_AR: هل contractor reports include device status, activity, trend observations, chemicals used, recommendations and corrective actions?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Require structured reports; reject incomplete reports.

rules.Question: Is contractor response time defined and monitored for urgent findings?
rules.Requirement Logic: High-risk pest incidents require defined response time and escalation if delayed.
rules.Corrective Action: Track response time; escalate delays; review contractor performance.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Delay for critical incident can keep product/area on hold.
rules.Question_AR: Is contractor response time defined and monitored for urgent findings?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Track response time; escalate delays; review contractor performance.

rules.Question: Is contractor performance reviewed against trends, findings, missed visits and corrective action effectiveness?
rules.Requirement Logic: Contractor performance must be evaluated by results and trends, not visits alone.
rules.Corrective Action: Review KPIs; issue CAPA; change provider if ineffective.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Ineffective service with increasing trends escalates module risk.
rules.Question_AR: Is contractor performance reviewed against trends, findings, missed visits and corrective action effectiveness?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Review KPIs; issue CAPA; change provider if ineffective.

rules.Question: Do pest records include date, device ID, location, pest type, count, action, responsible person and closure?
rules.Requirement Logic: Records must support trend analysis and auditability.
rules.Corrective Action: Update templates; train users; verify completeness.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Incomplete critical incident records may trigger M07 fail.
rules.Question_AR: هل pest records include date, device ID, location, pest type, count, action, responsible person and closure?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Update templates; train users; verify completeness.

rules.Question: Can pest incidents in production/storage be linked to batch, time window, line, shift or affected stock?
rules.Requirement Logic: High-risk pest incidents must support product impact assessment and recall scope.
rules.Corrective Action: Link incident to production time/line/batch/stock; quarantine if uncertain.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No linkage after high-risk incident → broad hold/recall scope.
rules.Question_AR: Can pest incidents in production/storage be linked to batch, time window, line, shift or affected stock?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Link incident to production time/line/batch/stock; quarantine if uncertain.

rules.Question: Are pesticide applications documented with chemical, dose, location, applicator, time and release status?
rules.Requirement Logic: Pesticide records must prove safe controlled use and support release decisions.
rules.Corrective Action: Document application; verify release; maintain SDS/approval.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Incomplete chemical record in food area may trigger product hold.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M03 chemical; M07 records; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: هل pesticide applications documented with chemical, dose, location, applicator, time and release status?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Document application; verify release; maintain SDS/approval.

rules.Question: Is the device register reconciled with the physical site and service records?
rules.Requirement Logic: All devices on site must match register, map and service records.
rules.Corrective Action: Reconcile map/register; remove ghost devices; add missing devices.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Missing devices in high-risk area may escalate to Major.
rules.Question_AR: هل device register reconciled with the physical site and service records?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Reconcile map/register; remove ghost devices; add missing devices.

rules.Question: Are pest corrective actions documented with root cause, action, owner, due date and effectiveness verification?
rules.Requirement Logic: Corrective actions must be complete and traceable to findings/trends.
rules.Corrective Action: Complete CAPA fields and effectiveness review.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Open overdue critical CAPA escalates risk.
rules.Question_AR: هل pest corrective actions documented with root cause, action, owner, due date and effectiveness verification?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Complete CAPA fields and effectiveness review.

rules.Question: For RTE or exposed finished product areas, is any internal pest activity escalated to product impact assessment?
rules.Requirement Logic: RTE/open finished product areas require stricter response and zero tolerance logic.
rules.Product / Process Trigger: Any pest activity in RTE zone
rules.Corrective Action: Hold affected product if exposure possible; clean; investigate; trend.
rules.HACCP Trigger: RTE pest activity → HACCP/M08 trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M06 operational; M08 traceability; M03 cleaning
rules.Question_AR: For جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE) or exposed finished product areas, is any internal pest activity escalated to product impact assessment?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold affected product if exposure possible; clean; investigate; trend.

rules.Question: For dairy/high moisture zones, are drain flies/crawling insects and moisture sources specifically monitored?
rules.Requirement Logic: Dairy wet environments require attention to drains, moisture and insect activity.
rules.Corrective Action: Add drain/flying insect focus; fix moisture sources.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Persistent activity triggers M02 drainage/M03 sanitation review.
rules.Question_AR: For dairy/high moisture zones, are drain flies/crawling insects and moisture sources specifically monitored?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Add drain/flying insect focus; fix moisture sources.

rules.Question: For meat/poultry, are organic waste, blood/fat residues and by-products controlled to prevent pest attraction?
rules.Requirement Logic: Meat organic load attracts pests and requires strict waste/cleaning controls.
rules.Corrective Action: Increase waste removal/cleaning; control by-products and drains.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Organic waste pest activity may trigger product impact review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 waste; M03 cleaning; M06 operations
rules.Question_AR: For meat/poultry, are organic waste, blood/fat residues and by-products controlled to prevent pest attraction?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Increase waste removal/cleaning; control by-products and drains.

rules.Question: For grain/powder/spice facilities, are stored-product insects controlled through receiving checks, storage rotation and pheromone monitoring where relevant?
rules.Requirement Logic: Dry ingredient facilities require specific controls for weevils/moths/beetles and infested lots.
rules.Corrective Action: Quarantine infested lots; inspect adjacent stock; review supplier/storage.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Infested lot → M01 supplier and M08 traceability trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M01 supplier; M06 storage; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: For grain/powder/spice facilities, are stored-product insects controlled through receiving checks, storage rotation and pheromone monitoring where relevant?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Quarantine infested lots; inspect adjacent stock; review supplier/storage.

rules.Question: For fish operations, are ice machines, wet tables, drains and dispatch areas monitored for insects/rodents/birds?
rules.Requirement Logic: Fish wet/ice areas and dispatch zones require specific pest focus.
rules.Corrective Action: Add focused monitoring; control drains/ice/waste/doors.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Activity near ice/open fish → Critical product impact review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 water/ice/drainage; M03 cleaning; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: For fish operations, are ice machines, wet tables, drains and dispatch areas monitored for insects/rodents/birds?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Add focused monitoring; control drains/ice/waste/doors.

rules.Question: For large warehouses or packaging stores, are perimeter, racking, dock doors and stock rotation checked for pest harbourage?
rules.Requirement Logic: Storage-heavy sites need warehouse-specific pest prevention and stock inspection.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Pest harbourage in racking/stock, packaging damage
rules.Corrective Action: Improve stock spacing, inspections, dock controls and FIFO.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Pest evidence in finished goods warehouse triggers M08/stock hold.
rules.Question_AR: For large warehouses or packaging stores, are perimeter, racking, dock doors and stock rotation checked for pest harbourage?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Improve stock spacing, inspections, dock controls and FIFO.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Pest harbourage in racking/stock, packaging damage

rules.Question: Is pest control included in internal GMP audits with verification of devices, records, trends and corrective actions?
rules.Requirement Logic: Internal audits should verify the pest control system beyond contractor visits.
rules.Corrective Action: Add pest system audit; close findings; trend audit results.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated audit failures escalate master GMP risk.
rules.Question_AR: Is pest control included in internal الممارسات الصحية الجيدة (GMP) audits with verification of devices, records, trends and corrective actions?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Add pest system audit; close findings; trend audit results.

rules.Question: Is the site able to simulate a pest incident and identify affected area, stock, cleaning action and corrective action path?
rules.Requirement Logic: A mock pest incident validates response speed and traceability.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Unprepared response to critical pest incident
rules.Corrective Action: Run mock test; improve response forms, batch linkage and escalation.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Failure may escalate to Major if traceability cannot identify affected stock.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 corrective action/documentation; EU traceability support
rules.Question_AR: هل site able to simulate a pest incident and identify affected area, stock, cleaning action and corrective action path?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Run mock test; improve response forms, batch linkage and escalation.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Unprepared response to critical pest incident

rules.Question: Are pest KPIs tracked such as activity trend, response time, overdue CAPA, missed inspections and repeat findings?
rules.Requirement Logic: KPIs convert pest control into measurable system performance.
rules.Corrective Action: Define KPIs and review monthly; link to contractor/internal performance.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Poor KPIs with increasing trends escalate risk.
rules.Question_AR: هل pest KPIs tracked such as activity trend, response time, overdue CAPA, missed inspections and repeat findings?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define KPIs and review monthly; link to contractor/internal performance.

rules.Question: Are employees trained to recognize and report pest evidence, and to avoid practices that attract pests?
rules.Requirement Logic: Employee awareness supports early detection and prevention.
rules.Corrective Action: Train staff; include waste/doors/storage/reporting practices.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Poor awareness links to M05 training.
rules.Question_AR: هل employees trained to recognize and report pest evidence, and to avoid practices that attract pests?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Train staff; include waste/doors/storage/reporting practices.

rules.Question: Is there a documented personnel hygiene policy covering health, hand hygiene, clothing, behavior, visitors and contractors?
rules.Requirement Logic: A documented policy must define hygiene requirements and responsibilities for all persons entering food areas.
rules.Corrective Action: Create and approve personnel hygiene policy; train all relevant personnel.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No personnel hygiene system = GMP fail; HACCP cannot rely on personnel controls.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal hygiene/training; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجد إجراء موثق لـ personnel hygiene policy covering health, hand hygiene, clothing, behavior, visitors and contractors?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Create and approve personnel hygiene policy; train all relevant personnel.

rules.Question: Are roles and areas classified by hygiene risk such as low-risk warehouse, open food handling, RTE/high hygiene, allergen handling?
rules.Requirement Logic: Personnel controls must be risk-based according to product exposure and role.
rules.Corrective Action: Classify roles/areas and apply controls by risk.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Weak classification triggers M02 zoning and M06 operation review.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 risk-based GHP; EU 852/2004 hygiene procedures
rules.Question_AR: هل roles and areas classified by hygiene risk such as low-risk warehouse, open food handling, جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE)/high hygiene, allergen handling?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Classify roles/areas and apply controls by risk.

rules.Question: Are responsibilities for hygiene monitoring, enforcement and release assigned to supervisors/QA?
rules.Requirement Logic: Personnel hygiene must be supervised and enforced, not left voluntary.
rules.Corrective Action: Assign supervisors/verifiers; train them; define escalation.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated noncompliance may trigger M07/Master risk escalation.
rules.Question_AR: هل responsibilities for hygiene monitoring, enforcement and release assigned to supervisors/QA?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Assign supervisors/verifiers; train them; define escalation.

rules.Question: Are hygiene rules communicated visibly at relevant entry points and work areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Rules should be understandable and visible where actions are required.
rules.Product / Process Trigger: Entry points, handwash stations, gowning areas
rules.Corrective Action: Post/update signs; verify understanding.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated behavior failures may escalate to training review.
rules.Question_AR: هل hygiene rules communicated visibly at relevant entry points and work areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Post/update signs; verify understanding.

rules.Question: Is there an escalation process for repeated hygiene violations?
rules.Requirement Logic: Repeated violations must trigger retraining, restriction or disciplinary action based on risk.
rules.Corrective Action: Define escalation; retrain/restrict; verify behavior change.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated critical behavior in RTE/open product areas may trigger product risk review.
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجدn escalation process for repeated hygiene violations?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define escalation; retrain/restrict; verify behavior change.

rules.Question: Is there a system requiring food handlers to report illness symptoms relevant to food safety?
rules.Requirement Logic: Food handlers must report symptoms that could transmit disease through food.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Norovirus, Salmonella, E. coli, Staph transmission
rules.Corrective Action: Implement illness reporting; educate staff; restrict affected workers.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Sick worker handling food may trigger product hold/traceability review.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 health status; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجد system requiring food handlers to report illness symptoms relevant to food safety?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Implement illness reporting; educate staff; restrict affected workers.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Norovirus, Salmonella, E. coli, Staph transmission

rules.Question: Are workers with vomiting, diarrhea, fever, infected wounds or suspected contagious illness excluded from food handling?
rules.Requirement Logic: Symptomatic or infected workers must not handle food or food-contact surfaces.
rules.Corrective Action: Remove/restrict worker; assess product/batch exposure; clean affected areas.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Ill worker handled exposed product → product impact assessment and possible M08 trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M08 traceability; M03 cleaning; M06 operational
rules.Question_AR: هل workers with vomiting, diarrhea, fever, infected wounds or suspected contagious illness excluded from food handling?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Remove/restrict worker; assess product/batch exposure; clean affected areas.

rules.Question: Are return-to-work criteria defined after illness or injury?
rules.Requirement Logic: Workers should return only when symptoms resolve and any clearance criteria are met.
rules.Corrective Action: Define clearance criteria and supervisor/QA approval.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No criteria may lead to recurrent health risk.
rules.Question_AR: هل return-to-work criteria defined after illness or injury?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define clearance criteria and supervisor/QA approval.

rules.Question: Are cuts, sores and wounds covered with suitable detectable waterproof dressings and gloves where needed?
rules.Requirement Logic: Wounds must be controlled to prevent blood, pus, or dressing contamination.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Staph contamination, blood, foreign body dressing
rules.Corrective Action: Cover wound; restrict if cannot be controlled; replace/check dressings.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Uncontrolled wound in food handling → product impact review.
rules.Question_AR: هل cuts, sores and wounds covered with suitable detectable waterproof dressings and gloves where needed?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Cover wound; restrict if cannot be controlled; replace/check dressings.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Staph contamination, blood, foreign body dressing

rules.Question: Are medical conditions or medications that may impair hygiene or contaminate food managed confidentially and risk-based?
rules.Requirement Logic: Food safety-relevant conditions must be managed without unnecessary personal data exposure.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Unexpected contamination or inability to follow hygiene
rules.Corrective Action: Risk assess work assignment; protect privacy; restrict where necessary.
rules.HACCP Trigger: If direct contamination risk occurs → product impact assessment.
rules.Question_AR: هل medical conditions or medications that may impair hygiene or contaminate food managed confidentially and risk-based?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Risk assess work assignment; protect privacy; restrict where necessary.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Unexpected contamination or inability to follow hygiene

rules.Question: Are first aid materials controlled so they do not contaminate food or food-contact areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: First aid kits/dressings must be controlled, clean, and suitable for food areas.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Foreign body and contamination from first aid materials
rules.Corrective Action: Use detectable dressings; control kit contents; inspect regularly.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Lost dressing in production → product hold/foreign body review.
rules.Question_AR: هل first aid materials controlled so they do not contaminate food or food-contact areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Use detectable dressings; control kit contents; inspect regularly.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Foreign body and contamination from first aid materials

rules.Question: Is handwashing required and performed at defined critical points?
rules.Requirement Logic: Handwashing must occur before food handling, after toilet, breaks, raw handling, waste, cleaning, touching face, and contamination events.
rules.Corrective Action: Retrain; increase supervision; stop high-risk handling if noncompliance observed.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Handwashing failure in RTE/open product → product risk assessment.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 handwash facilities; M06 operational; M08 traceability
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal cleanliness; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Question_AR: Is handwashing required and performed at defined critical points?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Retrain; increase supervision; stop high-risk handling if noncompliance observed.

rules.Question: Is the handwashing method defined and effective including wetting, soap, scrubbing, rinsing and hygienic drying?
rules.Requirement Logic: Method must be defined so handwashing is effective, not symbolic.
rules.Corrective Action: Train and verify method; correct poor practice.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated poor method triggers training/CAPA.
rules.Question_AR: هل handwashing method defined and effective including wetting, soap, scrubbing, rinsing and hygienic drying?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Train and verify method; correct poor practice.

rules.Question: Are handwashing stations functional, accessible and supplied with soap and hygienic drying?
rules.Requirement Logic: Facilities must support effective hand hygiene in relevant locations.
rules.Corrective Action: Supply/repair station; restrict handling until corrected.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No functional handwash for open food → GMP fail and M02 trigger.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal hygiene; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Notes: M02 has infrastructure; M05 verifies use/effectiveness.
rules.Question_AR: هل handwashing stations functional, accessible and supplied with soap and hygienic drying?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Supply/repair station; restrict handling until corrected.

rules.Question: If hand sanitizer is used, is it supplementary to handwashing and suitable for food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Sanitizer does not replace handwashing when hands are soiled; product suitability must be controlled.
rules.Hazard / Risk: False sense of hygiene, chemical contamination
rules.Corrective Action: Clarify sanitizer use; approve products; train staff.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Misuse in RTE/open handling may trigger observation failure.
rules.Question_AR: If hand sanitizer is used, is it supplementary to handwashing and suitable for food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Clarify sanitizer use; approve products; train staff.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: False sense of hygiene, chemical contamination

rules.Question: Are gloves controlled as a hygiene barrier rather than assumed safe?
rules.Requirement Logic: Gloves must be clean, intact, changed at defined points and not replace hand hygiene.
rules.Corrective Action: Define change points; train staff; monitor glove integrity/use.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Glove misuse in RTE/open product can escalate to Critical.
rules.Question_AR: هل gloves controlled as a hygiene barrier rather than assumed safe?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define change points; train staff; monitor glove integrity/use.

rules.Question: For RTE/exposed finished foods, is bare-hand contact prevented or strictly controlled?
rules.Requirement Logic: RTE handling must minimize direct hand contact through utensils/gloves and strict hygiene controls.
rules.Corrective Action: Use utensils/gloves; retrain; assess product exposure.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Bare-hand RTE contact without control → product impact assessment.
rules.Question_AR: For جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE)/exposed finished foods, is bare-hand contact prevented or strictly controlled?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Use utensils/gloves; retrain; assess product exposure.

rules.Question: Is hand hygiene compliance verified by observation or other methods in high-risk areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: High-risk operations require verification that hand hygiene procedures are followed.
rules.Corrective Action: Implement observations; trend noncompliance; retrain.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated failures escalate M05 and M06 risk.
rules.Question_AR: Is hand hygiene compliance verified by observation or other methods in high-risk areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Implement observations; trend noncompliance; retrain.

rules.Question: Are food handlers provided with clean protective clothing appropriate to risk?
rules.Requirement Logic: Protective clothing must prevent contamination from personal clothing and body sources.
rules.Corrective Action: Provide clean clothing; define change frequency and storage.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Dirty clothing in high hygiene/RTE may escalate to Critical.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal cleanliness; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Question_AR: هل food handlers provided with clean protective clothing appropriate to risk?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Provide clean clothing; define change frequency and storage.

rules.Question: Are hair and beard restraints used where exposed product or food-contact surfaces may be contaminated?
rules.Requirement Logic: Hair/beard restraint must prevent physical contamination.
rules.Corrective Action: Provide hair/beard covers; retrain; enforce at entry.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Hair in product/contact area triggers foreign matter assessment.
rules.Question_AR: هل hair and beard restraints used where exposed product or food-contact surfaces may be contaminated?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Provide hair/beard covers; retrain; enforce at entry.

rules.Question: Is footwear controlled to prevent transfer between raw/dirty and clean/high hygiene areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Footwear must be dedicated/cleaned/changed where zone transfer creates risk.
rules.Corrective Action: Use dedicated footwear/boot wash; enforce zoning.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Failure in RTE/high hygiene may trigger M02/M06 review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 zoning; M03 cleaning; M06 operational
rules.Question_AR: Is footwear controlled to prevent transfer between raw/dirty and clean/high hygiene areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Use dedicated footwear/boot wash; enforce zoning.

rules.Question: Are PPE change points and frequency defined based on contamination risk?
rules.Requirement Logic: PPE must be changed after contamination, breaks, raw handling or zone changes as appropriate.
rules.Corrective Action: Define change points; provide PPE; monitor compliance.
rules.HACCP Trigger: PPE carryover in high-risk areas may trigger product impact review.
rules.Question_AR: هل معدات الحماية الشخصية (PPE) change points and frequency defined based on contamination risk?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define change points; provide PPE; monitor compliance.

rules.Question: Is clean/dirty clothing storage separated and protected from contamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: Clean protective clothing must not be stored with dirty clothing or personal items.
rules.Corrective Action: Separate storage; improve laundry control.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated failure triggers M02 hygiene facility review.
rules.Notes: May become Major in high hygiene.
rules.Question_AR: Is clean/dirty clothing storage separated and protected from contamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Separate storage; improve laundry control.

rules.Question: Is protective clothing laundered or controlled to avoid cross-contamination and foreign matter?
rules.Requirement Logic: Laundry controls should ensure clothing is clean and suitable before use.
rules.Corrective Action: Define laundry method; inspect returns; segregate dirty/clean.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Dirty workwear in RTE/high hygiene may trigger high-risk finding.
rules.Question_AR: Is protective clothing laundered or controlled to avoid cross-contamination and foreign matter?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define laundry method; inspect returns; segregate dirty/clean.

rules.Question: Are jewelry, watches, phones and personal items controlled in food handling areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Personal items that can contaminate food or distract hygiene behavior must be restricted.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Foreign body, microbial contamination from phones/jewelry
rules.Corrective Action: Remove unauthorized items; provide storage; retrain staff.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Foreign body incident may trigger M08 traceability review.
rules.Question_AR: هل jewelry, watches, phones and personal items controlled in food handling areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Remove unauthorized items; provide storage; retrain staff.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Foreign body, microbial contamination from phones/jewelry

rules.Question: Are eating, drinking, chewing gum, smoking/vaping prohibited in food handling and storage areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Activities that contaminate food or hands must be restricted to designated areas.
rules.Corrective Action: Enforce designated areas; retrain; escalate repeats.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Violation in open product area may trigger product exposure review.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal behavior; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Question_AR: هل eating, drinking, chewing gum, smoking/vaping prohibited in food handling and storage areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Enforce designated areas; retrain; escalate repeats.

rules.Question: Are behaviors like touching face, hair, phone, waste or dirty surfaces followed by handwashing before food handling?
rules.Requirement Logic: Behavioral contamination events must trigger hand hygiene before returning to food handling.
rules.Corrective Action: Retrain and monitor; stop/reclean hands before continuing.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated failure in RTE/open product escalates to Critical.
rules.Question_AR: هل behaviors like touching face, hair, phone, waste or dirty surfaces followed by handwashing before food handling?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Retrain and monitor; stop/reclean hands before continuing.

rules.Question: Are workers prevented from moving from raw/dirty tasks to RTE/clean tasks without required hygiene change?
rules.Requirement Logic: Raw-to-clean movement must be controlled through handwashing/PPE/footwear/clothing change or dedicated personnel.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Cross-contamination between raw and RTE/finished product
rules.Corrective Action: Restrict movement; implement change procedure; assess product exposure.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Raw-to-RTE movement without hygiene control → product impact assessment.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 layout; M06 operational; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: هل workers prevented from moving from raw/dirty tasks to جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE)/clean tasks without required hygiene change?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Restrict movement; implement change procedure; assess product exposure.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Cross-contamination between raw and RTE/finished product

rules.Question: Are workers trained and controlled to prevent allergen cross-contact during handling, cleaning and changeovers?
rules.Requirement Logic: Allergen behavior controls must prevent transfer through hands, gloves, tools and clothing.
rules.Corrective Action: Use dedicated PPE/tools or change procedures; verify cleaning.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Allergen transfer risk → label/traceability trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M03 allergen cleaning; M08 traceability; label engine
rules.Question_AR: هل workers trained and controlled to prevent allergen cross-contact during handling, cleaning and changeovers?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Use dedicated PPE/tools or change procedures; verify cleaning.

rules.Question: Are workers handling waste required to change gloves/wash hands/change PPE before handling food or packaging?
rules.Requirement Logic: Waste handling can contaminate hands/PPE and must be separated from food handling.
rules.Corrective Action: Define waste handling controls; retrain; separate roles where possible.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Waste-to-food behavior in RTE/open product may escalate to Critical.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 waste; M03 cleaning; M06 operational
rules.Question_AR: هل workers handling waste required to change gloves/wash hands/change معدات الحماية الشخصية (PPE) before handling food or packaging?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define waste handling controls; retrain; separate roles where possible.

rules.Question: Do workers use tools/utensils in a way that prevents cross-contamination between zones/products?
rules.Requirement Logic: Utensil/tool use must follow zoning and cleaning rules.
rules.Corrective Action: Retrain; enforce color coding/dedicated tools.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Wrong tool use may trigger M03 cleaning and M06 hold if product exposed.
rules.Question_AR: هل workers use tools/utensils in a way that prevents cross-contamination between zones/products?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Retrain; enforce color coding/dedicated tools.

rules.Question: Do all food handlers receive food safety and hygiene induction before starting work?
rules.Requirement Logic: Personnel must be trained or supervised to perform hygiene tasks safely before food handling.
rules.Corrective Action: Train before assignment; restrict untrained workers.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Untrained worker in high-risk handling may trigger product exposure review.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 training; EU 852/2004 Annex II Ch. XII
rules.Question_AR: هل all food handlers receive food safety and hygiene induction before starting work?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Train before assignment; restrict untrained workers.

rules.Question: Is training specific to the worker's role, product risk, zone, equipment and hygiene controls?
rules.Requirement Logic: Training must cover role-specific hazards and expected behaviors, not generic awareness only.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Wrong practice due to incomplete training
rules.Corrective Action: Build training matrix; assign modules by role/zone/product.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Training gaps trigger M07 and Master Engine scoring.
rules.Question_AR: Is training specific to the worker's role, product risk, zone, equipment and hygiene controls?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Build training matrix; assign modules by role/zone/product.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Wrong practice due to incomplete training

rules.Question: Is refresher training provided at defined intervals and after deviations?
rules.Requirement Logic: Training must be refreshed and triggered by hygiene failures, changes or trends.
rules.Corrective Action: Schedule refresher; retrain after violations/deviations.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated behavior failures without retraining → M05 weakness.
rules.Question_AR: Is refresher training provided at defined intervals and after deviations?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Schedule refresher; retrain after violations/deviations.

rules.Question: Is training effectiveness verified through observation, test or supervisor assessment?
rules.Requirement Logic: Attendance alone is insufficient; competence must be verified where risk requires.
rules.Corrective Action: Assess competency; retrain/restrict if failed.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Critical task incompetence may trigger product risk if already assigned.
rules.Question_AR: Is training effectiveness verified through observation, test or supervisor assessment?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Assess competency; retrain/restrict if failed.

rules.Question: Are temporary, seasonal and agency workers controlled with the same hygiene training and supervision?
rules.Requirement Logic: Non-permanent workers must not bypass hygiene controls.
rules.Corrective Action: Train before work; assign supervision; monitor compliance.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Untrained temporary worker in high-risk task → critical review.
rules.Question_AR: هل temporary, seasonal and agency workers controlled with the same hygiene training and supervision?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Train before work; assign supervision; monitor compliance.

rules.Question: Are contractors and visitors briefed and controlled before entering food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Visitors/contractors must follow site hygiene and be escorted or restricted as needed.
rules.Corrective Action: Brief visitors; provide PPE; restrict access; escort.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Visitor contamination in food area may trigger product exposure review.
rules.Question_AR: هل contractors and visitors briefed and controlled before entering food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Brief visitors; provide PPE; restrict access; escort.

rules.Question: Are changing rooms used to separate personal clothing/items from workwear and food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Workers should change/store clothing to prevent contamination entering food areas.
rules.Corrective Action: Enforce changing process; separate personal items.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Poor facility design/use triggers M02 hygiene facility review.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal hygiene; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Notes: M02 provides facility; M05 verifies use.
rules.Question_AR: هل changing rooms used to separate personal clothing/items from workwear and food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Enforce changing process; separate personal items.

rules.Question: Are workers required to wash hands and control PPE/clothing after using toilets before re-entering food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Toilet-to-production transition must prevent fecal contamination.
rules.Corrective Action: Enforce handwashing/change controls; retrain; monitor entry.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Failure with open/RTE product may trigger product impact assessment.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 personal cleanliness; EU 852/2004 Annex II
rules.Question_AR: هل workers required to wash hands and control معدات الحماية الشخصية (PPE)/clothing after using toilets before re-entering food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Enforce handwashing/change controls; retrain; monitor entry.

rules.Question: Are eating/drinking/break areas separated from food handling and storage areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Break areas must prevent food handler contamination and personal items entering food zones.
rules.Corrective Action: Separate break areas; enforce handwashing before return.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Poor design triggers M02 review; behavior violations trigger M05 CAPA.
rules.Question_AR: هل eating/drinking/break areas separated from food handling and storage areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Separate break areas; enforce handwashing before return.

rules.Question: Are routine hygiene inspections performed for hands, clothing, PPE, behavior and zone compliance?
rules.Requirement Logic: Routine observation detects behavior issues before contamination occurs.
rules.Corrective Action: Implement routine checks; record and trend findings.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Recurring findings trigger CAPA/training.
rules.Question_AR: هل routine hygiene inspections performed for hands, clothing, معدات الحماية الشخصية (PPE), behavior and zone compliance?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Implement routine checks; record and trend findings.

rules.Question: Is personnel hygiene checked before starting high-risk/RTE/open product operations?
rules.Requirement Logic: High-risk operations should include personnel readiness checks before line start.
rules.Corrective Action: Check clothing/hands/PPE/health status before line start.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Failed check before production → block start; after start → product impact assessment.
rules.Question_AR: Is personnel hygiene checked before starting high-risk/جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE)/open product operations?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Check clothing/hands/PPE/health status before line start.

rules.Question: Are hygiene nonconformances recorded, trended and closed with corrective actions?
rules.Requirement Logic: Behavior findings must be managed through CAPA and trend analysis.
rules.Corrective Action: Record, assign root cause/action, verify effectiveness.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Increasing trend escalates module risk.
rules.Question_AR: هل hygiene nonconformances recorded, trended and closed with corrective actions?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Record, assign root cause/action, verify effectiveness.

rules.Question: Are personnel hygiene observations trended by area, shift, role and violation type?
rules.Requirement Logic: Trend analysis identifies weak shifts, roles or behaviors.
rules.Corrective Action: Trend by shift/area/behavior; target training/enforcement.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Increasing trend triggers Master Engine risk escalation.
rules.Question_AR: هل personnel hygiene observations trended by area, shift, role and violation type?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Trend by shift/area/behavior; target training/enforcement.

rules.Question: Do supervisors verify hygiene compliance during operations, not only during audits?
rules.Requirement Logic: Supervision during work is needed to control real-time behavior.
rules.Corrective Action: Define supervisor checks; coach/correct immediately.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Lack of supervision in high-risk handling may escalate.
rules.Question_AR: هل supervisors verify hygiene compliance during operations, not only during audits?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define supervisor checks; coach/correct immediately.

rules.Question: Is there a worker register with unique Worker ID, role, training status and hygiene restrictions?
rules.Requirement Logic: Each worker involved in food operations should be identifiable for training and traceability.
rules.Corrective Action: Create Worker ID system; maintain role/training/restriction status.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No worker ID weakens M08 traceability and training control.
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجد worker register with unique Worker ID, role, training status and hygiene restrictions?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Create Worker ID system; maintain role/training/restriction status.

rules.Question: Can workers assigned to each batch/line/shift be identified?
rules.Requirement Logic: Worker-to-batch linkage supports investigation after illness, hygiene deviation or contamination event.
rules.Corrective Action: Record worker IDs by batch/line/shift/time; integrate with M08.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No worker-batch link after critical hygiene incident → broad product hold/recall scope.
rules.Question_AR: Can workers assigned to each batch/line/shift be identified?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Record worker IDs by batch/line/shift/time; integrate with M08.

rules.Question: Are high-risk tasks assigned only to trained/approved workers?
rules.Requirement Logic: Critical tasks must be performed by workers with verified training and competence.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Unqualified worker contaminates product or fails control
rules.Product / Process Trigger: RTE handling, allergen changeover, sanitation release, CCP monitoring
rules.Corrective Action: Restrict task to authorized workers; retrain/verify competence.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Unauthorized worker on high-risk task → product/process review.
rules.Question_AR: هل high-risk tasks assigned only to trained/approved workers?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Restrict task to authorized workers; retrain/verify competence.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Unqualified worker contaminates product or fails control

rules.Question: If a worker reports illness after a shift, can affected products/batches be identified?
rules.Requirement Logic: Illness reports must be linked to shift/line/batches for product risk assessment.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Cannot assess product impact from sick worker
rules.Corrective Action: Identify batches/time windows; assess risk; hold if needed.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Sick worker after food handling → M08 product impact trigger.
rules.Question_AR: If a worker reports illness after a shift, can affected products/batches be identified?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Identify batches/time windows; assess risk; hold if needed.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Cannot assess product impact from sick worker

rules.Question: Can hygiene incidents be linked to affected line, batch, time window and workers?
rules.Requirement Logic: Hygiene incidents require traceability for product disposition decisions.
rules.Corrective Action: Record line/time/batch/worker; hold affected scope if uncertain.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Hygiene incident with product exposure → M08 trigger.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 documentation/corrective action; EU traceability support
rules.Question_AR: Can hygiene incidents be linked to affected line, batch, time window and workers?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Record line/time/batch/worker; hold affected scope if uncertain.

rules.Question: Are visitors/contractors entering food areas logged with date, time, area and escort?
rules.Requirement Logic: Visitors/contractors must be traceable if contamination investigation is needed.
rules.Corrective Action: Log visitor identity/time/area/escort; apply hygiene briefing.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Unlogged visitor in high-risk area may trigger security/hygiene review.
rules.Question_AR: هل visitors/contractors entering food areas logged with date, time, area and escort?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Log visitor identity/time/area/escort; apply hygiene briefing.

rules.Question: Are personal items that may become foreign bodies controlled before entering food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Items such as pens, badges, clips, false nails/eyelashes must be controlled based on risk.
rules.Corrective Action: Remove/replace with controlled/detectable items; retrain.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Foreign body incident triggers product traceability review.
rules.Question_AR: هل personal items that may become foreign bodies controlled before entering food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Remove/replace with controlled/detectable items; retrain.

rules.Question: Are fingernails kept clean/short and artificial nails/nail polish controlled where food is handled?
rules.Requirement Logic: Nail controls prevent physical and biological contamination.
rules.Corrective Action: Enforce nail policy; restrict if noncompliant.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Noncompliance in RTE/open product may escalate.
rules.Question_AR: هل fingernails kept clean/short and artificial nails/nail polish controlled where food is handled?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Enforce nail policy; restrict if noncompliant.

rules.Question: Where unavoidable items are used in production, are they controlled/detectable and accounted for?
rules.Requirement Logic: Necessary items should be detectable, controlled and checked to prevent loss into product.
rules.Corrective Action: Use detectable/controlled items; investigate losses.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Lost item in product area → product hold and search/trace.
rules.Question_AR: Where unavoidable items are used in production, are they controlled/detectable and accounted for?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Use detectable/controlled items; investigate losses.

rules.Question: For RTE/exposed finished products, are personnel controls stricter than general processing areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: RTE/high hygiene areas require strict entry, hand hygiene, clothing and movement controls.
rules.Corrective Action: Strengthen access, hand hygiene, PPE, movement controls.
rules.HACCP Trigger: RTE personnel failure → product impact assessment.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 zoning; M06 operational; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: For جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE)/exposed finished products, are personnel controls stricter than general processing areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Strengthen access, hand hygiene, PPE, movement controls.

rules.Question: For meat/poultry, are workers controlled to prevent raw-to-cooked/RTE cross-contamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: Raw/cooked worker movement must be separated or controlled by full hygiene change.
rules.Corrective Action: Separate personnel or enforce full change; assess exposure.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Raw-to-cooked movement failure → M08 product risk trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 layout; M06 operational; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: For meat/poultry, are workers controlled to prevent raw-to-cooked/جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE) cross-contamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Separate personnel or enforce full change; assess exposure.

rules.Question: For dairy/high-moisture operations, are worker practices controlled to avoid post-clean/refrigerated area contamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: Dairy personnel controls should address wet areas, drains, footwear, condensation and high hygiene entries.
rules.Corrective Action: Strengthen boot/hand/PPE controls; monitor wet zone movement.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Persistent failures trigger M03/M02 environmental review.
rules.Question_AR: For dairy/high-moisture operations, are worker practices controlled to avoid post-clean/refrigerated area contamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Strengthen boot/hand/PPE controls; monitor wet zone movement.

rules.Question: For fish operations, are workers handling ice/fish required to prevent contamination of ice, wet surfaces and product?
rules.Requirement Logic: Ice and wet handling require strict hand/tool/PPE hygiene.
rules.Corrective Action: Control ice handling tools/hands/PPE; retrain staff.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Contaminated ice/product → M08 traceability review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 ice/water; M03 cleaning; M06 operations
rules.Question_AR: For fish operations, are workers handling ice/fish required to prevent contamination of ice, wet surfaces and product?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Control ice handling tools/hands/PPE; retrain staff.

rules.Question: For dry products, are personnel practices controlled to prevent moisture introduction, allergen/dust transfer and foreign bodies?
rules.Requirement Logic: Dry operations require controls for personal items, dust/allergen transfer and moisture introduction.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Moisture, dust, allergen carryover, foreign bodies
rules.Corrective Action: Control personal items, water use, PPE/tool zoning.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Moisture/allergen/foreign body failures trigger M03/M06.
rules.Question_AR: For dry products, are personnel practices controlled to prevent moisture introduction, allergen/dust transfer and foreign bodies?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Control personal items, water use, PPE/tool zoning.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Moisture, dust, allergen carryover, foreign bodies

rules.Question: For allergen handling, are personnel controls defined for allergen PPE, handwashing and movement before non-allergen handling?
rules.Requirement Logic: Allergen-handling personnel must prevent allergen transfer by hands, PPE, clothing and tools.
rules.Corrective Action: Enforce allergen change controls; validate cleaning and personnel change.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Allergen personnel failure → label/traceability trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M03 allergen cleaning; M08 traceability; label engine
rules.Question_AR: For allergen handling, are personnel controls defined for allergen معدات الحماية الشخصية (PPE), handwashing and movement before non-allergen handling?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Enforce allergen change controls; validate cleaning and personnel change.

rules.Question: Do personnel hygiene records include worker ID, date, area, observation, finding, action and verifier where relevant?
rules.Requirement Logic: Records must support auditability, trend and traceability.
rules.Corrective Action: Update templates and train supervisors.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Incomplete records weaken M07 and master score.
rules.Question_AR: هل personnel hygiene records include worker ID, date, area, observation, finding, action and verifier where relevant?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Update templates and train supervisors.

rules.Question: Are training records linked to Worker ID and job role?
rules.Requirement Logic: Training evidence must be worker-specific to support authorization and investigations.
rules.Corrective Action: Link training to Worker ID, role and expiry/refresher dates.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Training gap affects high-risk task assignment.
rules.Question_AR: هل training records linked to Worker ID and job role?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Link training to Worker ID, role and expiry/refresher dates.

rules.Question: Are illness, wound, restriction and return-to-work records maintained confidentially and linked to work assignment risk?
rules.Requirement Logic: Restriction records must support food safety decisions while limiting unnecessary personal details.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Uncontrolled restricted worker or privacy overcollection
rules.Corrective Action: Record food safety restriction status; protect privacy; link to assignment.
rules.HACCP Trigger: If restricted worker assigned to food handling → critical.
rules.Question_AR: هل illness, wound, restriction and return-to-work records maintained confidentially and linked to work assignment risk?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Record food safety restriction status; protect privacy; link to assignment.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Uncontrolled restricted worker or privacy overcollection

rules.Question: Is personnel hygiene system reviewed after illness event, hygiene trend, complaint, foreign body or product contamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: System must be reviewed after evidence of ineffective personnel controls.
rules.Corrective Action: Review root cause; update training/SOP/supervision; verify effectiveness.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated failures escalate master GMP risk.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 system review; EU 852/2004 HACCP procedures
rules.Question_AR: Is personnel hygiene system reviewed after illness event, hygiene trend, complaint, foreign body or product contamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Review root cause; update training/SOP/supervision; verify effectiveness.

rules.Question: Is worker-to-batch traceability tested through mock scenario?
rules.Requirement Logic: Mock testing validates ability to identify affected batches after worker illness or hygiene incident.
rules.Corrective Action: Run mock test; improve data fields and response time.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Failure may escalate if high-risk product traceability is weak.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 documentation/corrective action; EU traceability support
rules.Question_AR: Is worker-to-batch traceability tested through mock scenario?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Run mock test; improve data fields and response time.

rules.Question: Are food handlers required to step away, cover, and wash hands/change PPE after coughing/sneezing contamination events?
rules.Requirement Logic: Respiratory contamination events must be controlled to protect exposed food/surfaces.
rules.Corrective Action: Train response; replace gloves/PPE; assess exposed product if direct contamination occurred.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Direct contamination of RTE/open product → product impact assessment.
rules.Question_AR: هل food handlers required to step away, cover, and wash hands/change معدات الحماية الشخصية (PPE) after coughing/sneezing contamination events?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Train response; replace gloves/PPE; assess exposed product if direct contamination occurred.

rules.Question: Are personnel restricted from entering zones they are not authorized/trained for?
rules.Requirement Logic: Access must match hygiene zoning and training authorization.
rules.Corrective Action: Implement access controls; retrain; review affected zone if entry occurred.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Unauthorized entry into RTE/high hygiene may trigger product exposure review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 layout; M06 operational; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: هل personnel restricted from entering zones they are not authorized/trained for?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Implement access controls; retrain; review affected zone if entry occurred.

rules.Subcategory: Use of personal medication/cosmetics in production
rules.Question: Are cosmetics, perfumes, personal medications and similar items controlled in food areas?
rules.Requirement Logic: Personal products must not contaminate food or create chemical/foreign body risks.
rules.Corrective Action: Restrict personal products to lockers/designated areas.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Incident with product exposure escalates.
rules.Question_AR: هل cosmetics, perfumes, personal medications and similar items controlled in food areas?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Restrict personal products to lockers/designated areas.

rules.Question: If drinking water is allowed in certain areas, is it controlled to prevent contamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: Hydration policies must avoid open containers or personal bottles contaminating product areas.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Spills, foreign bodies, mouth contact contamination
rules.Applicability Rule: Hot environment=Yes OR water bottles allowed=Yes
rules.Corrective Action: Use designated controlled hydration points away from food/packaging.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Open bottle in food handling may escalate to Major.
rules.Active Input Trigger: Hot environment=Yes OR water bottles allowed=Yes
rules.Question_AR: If drinking water is allowed in certain areas, is it controlled to prevent contamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Use designated controlled hydration points away from food/packaging.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Spills, foreign bodies, mouth contact contamination

rules.Question: Are hygiene instructions understandable to workers in relevant languages or formats?
rules.Requirement Logic: Training and procedures must be understood by workers performing the tasks.
rules.Corrective Action: Translate/simplify/use visuals; verify understanding.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Misunderstanding in critical tasks may escalate.
rules.Question_AR: هل hygiene instructions understandable to workers in relevant languages or formats?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Translate/simplify/use visuals; verify understanding.

rules.Question: Are contractors performing maintenance/construction controlled to prevent contamination from tools, clothing, debris and behavior?
rules.Requirement Logic: Contractors must comply with food area hygiene and post-work release requirements.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Maintenance debris, chemical contamination, dirty clothing
rules.Corrective Action: Brief/escort contractors; cover product; clean/release area.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Contractor contamination event triggers M03/M08 review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M03 cleaning; M02 maintenance; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: هل contractors performing maintenance/construction controlled to prevent contamination from tools, clothing, debris and behavior?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Brief/escort contractors; cover product; clean/release area.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Maintenance debris, chemical contamination, dirty clothing

rules.Question: Are visitors entering food areas asked to comply with illness and hygiene restrictions?
rules.Requirement Logic: Visitors should not enter food areas if they pose food safety health risk.
rules.Corrective Action: Add health/hygiene declaration and access restriction.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Ill visitor in high hygiene/open product may trigger incident review.
rules.Question_AR: هل visitors entering food areas asked to comply with illness and hygiene restrictions?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Add health/hygiene declaration and access restriction.

rules.Question: Is there a documented process flow from receiving to finished product release?
rules.Requirement Logic: The process flow must identify all steps, inputs, outputs, delays, storage and rework loops.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Unidentified process hazards and uncontrolled steps
rules.Corrective Action: Create and verify process flow before risk assessment or operation.
rules.HACCP Trigger: No verified process flow undermines HACCP/GMP decision logic.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M01, M02, HACCP Module, Master Engine
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 HACCP preliminary steps/control measures; EU 852/2004 HACCP procedures
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجد إجراء موثق لـ process flow from receiving to finished product release?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Create and verify process flow before risk assessment or operation.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Unidentified process hazards and uncontrolled steps

rules.Question: Are operational parameters defined for each process step where safety or suitability is affected?
rules.Requirement Logic: Each relevant step must define limits/targets such as time, temperature, pH, aw, pressure, flow, concentration or humidity.
rules.Product / Process Trigger: Process step has food safety impact
rules.Corrective Action: Define parameters and acceptable limits for each relevant step.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Undefined safety parameter may become HACCP trigger.
rules.Question_AR: هل operational parameters defined for each process step where safety or suitability is affected?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Define parameters and acceptable limits for each relevant step.

rules.Question: Is there a product-process matrix linking product type, process type, hazard and required controls?
rules.Requirement Logic: The system must activate rules based on product and process risk, not generic checklist only.
rules.Corrective Action: Build matrix; link to M01-M08 and HACCP triggers.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Missing matrix weakens dynamic rule engine.
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 risk-based application; EU 852/2004 HACCP procedures
rules.Question_AR: هل يوجد product-process matrix linking product type, process type, hazard and required controls?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Build matrix; link to M01-M08 and HACCP triggers.

rules.Question: Are changes to product, process, equipment, layout or parameters assessed before implementation?
rules.Requirement Logic: Operational changes must trigger risk assessment, SOP update, training and HACCP review where relevant.
rules.Corrective Action: Assess impact; update SOPs; train; verify first production.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Major change may require HACCP plan review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 facility; M07 documentation; HACCP Module
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 review after changes; EU 852/2004 HACCP procedures
rules.Question_AR: هل changes to product, process, equipment, layout or parameters assessed before implementation?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Assess impact; update SOPs; train; verify first production.

rules.Question: Are work instructions available at point of use and understood by operators?
rules.Requirement Logic: Operators must have clear instructions for safe operation and monitoring.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Operator error due to unclear instructions
rules.Corrective Action: Create/update instructions; train; verify understanding.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Critical step instructions missing may trigger product hold/review.
rules.Question_AR: هل work instructions available at point of use and understood by operators?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Create/update instructions; train; verify understanding.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Operator error due to unclear instructions

rules.Question: Are receiving checks defined for raw materials, ingredients, packaging and utilities where relevant?
rules.Requirement Logic: Receiving must verify condition, identity, temperature, integrity, COA/testing and supplier approval as applicable.
rules.Corrective Action: Hold/reject materials not meeting criteria; update supplier risk.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Supplier weakness or critical raw material hazard may make receiving a CCP/OPRP.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M01 supplier; M08 traceability; HACCP Module
rules.Question_AR: هل receiving checks defined for raw materials, ingredients, packaging and utilities where relevant?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold/reject materials not meeting criteria; update supplier risk.

rules.Question: Are temperature-sensitive materials checked at receipt against defined limits?
rules.Requirement Logic: Chilled/frozen/hot materials must be assessed at receipt to prevent growth or safety loss.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Pathogen growth due to temperature abuse
rules.Corrective Action: Hold/reject if out of limit; assess time/temperature evidence.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated or severe receipt temp failure triggers supplier/HACCP review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M01 supplier; M08 traceability; HACCP Module
rules.Question_AR: هل temperature-sensitive materials checked at receipt against defined limits?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold/reject if out of limit; assess time/temperature evidence.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Pathogen growth due to temperature abuse

rules.Question: Are high-risk raw materials held until required COA/testing/inspection is acceptable?
rules.Requirement Logic: Materials with significant hazards must not enter production before release criteria are met.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Use of unsafe high-risk raw material
rules.Product / Process Trigger: Aflatoxin, antibiotics, pathogens, allergens, heavy metals
rules.Corrective Action: Hold material until evidence acceptable; block production use.
rules.HACCP Trigger: If supplier control weak, receiving may become CCP/OPRP.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M01 supplier; M08 traceability; HACCP Module
rules.Codex / EU Reference: CXC 1-1969 hazard control/receiving; EU 852/2004 HACCP procedures
rules.Question_AR: هل high-risk raw materials held until required شهادة التحليل (COA)/testing/inspection is acceptable?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold material until evidence acceptable; block production use.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Use of unsafe high-risk raw material

rules.Question: Is food-contact packaging inspected and protected before release to production?
rules.Requirement Logic: Primary packaging must be verified for integrity, cleanliness, identity and suitability.
rules.Corrective Action: Hold damaged/contaminated/wrong packaging; trace supplier.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Packaging issue affects M02 storage and M08 traceability.
rules.Question_AR: Is food-contact packaging inspected and protected before release to production?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold damaged/contaminated/wrong packaging; trace supplier.

rules.Question: Are delivery vehicles/containers assessed for cleanliness, pest evidence, temperature and segregation?
rules.Requirement Logic: Transport conditions must not introduce contamination or temperature abuse.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Contamination from vehicle or mixed loads
rules.Corrective Action: Reject/hold load if vehicle condition unacceptable; notify supplier.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Vehicle contamination may trigger M01 supplier review and material hold.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M01 supplier; M04 pest; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: هل delivery vehicles/containers assessed for cleanliness, pest evidence, temperature and segregation?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Reject/hold load if vehicle condition unacceptable; notify supplier.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Contamination from vehicle or mixed loads

rules.Question: Are operational temperature limits defined, monitored and recorded where safety depends on temperature?
rules.Requirement Logic: Temperature controls must have limits, monitoring frequency, device, responsible person and action for deviation.
rules.Sector: Dairy, Meat, Fish, RTE, Frozen, Chilled
rules.Corrective Action: Adjust/repair; hold affected product; assess time/temperature exposure.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Temperature deviation may be CCP/OPRP trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 utilities; M08 traceability; HACCP Module
rules.Question_AR: هل operational temperature limits defined, monitored and recorded where safety depends on temperature?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Adjust/repair; hold affected product; assess time/temperature exposure.

rules.Question: Are time limits defined and monitored where time outside control affects safety?
rules.Requirement Logic: Exposure, holding, cooling, thawing and staging times must be controlled where relevant.
rules.Corrective Action: Hold product if limit exceeded; assess safety; document disposition.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Time abuse with safety impact triggers HACCP/product hold.
rules.Question_AR: هل time limits defined and monitored where time outside control affects safety?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold product if limit exceeded; assess safety; document disposition.

rules.Question: Are pH limits defined, monitored and verified where pH controls safety or stability?
rules.Requirement Logic: pH must be controlled in acidified/fermented/low-acid-risk products according to process design.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Pathogen growth or toxin production due to wrong pH
rules.Corrective Action: Hold batch; adjust/reprocess only if validated; verify final pH.
rules.HACCP Trigger: pH deviation may be CCP/OPRP and release trigger.
rules.Linked Module Impact: HACCP Module; M07 records; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: هل pH limits defined, monitored and verified where pH controls safety or stability?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold batch; adjust/reprocess only if validated; verify final pH.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Pathogen growth or toxin production due to wrong pH

rules.Question: Are aw/moisture limits defined and monitored where water activity controls safety?
rules.Requirement Logic: Water activity/moisture must be controlled in dried, intermediate moisture and shelf-stable products.
rules.Sector: Grains, Dried foods, Confectionery, Intermediate moisture
rules.Corrective Action: Hold batch; continue drying/adjust only if validated; verify final aw.
rules.HACCP Trigger: aw deviation can trigger HACCP/product hold.
rules.Question_AR: هل aw/moisture limits defined and monitored where water activity controls safety?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold batch; continue drying/adjust only if validated; verify final aw.

rules.Question: Are process chemical concentrations controlled where used as safety/suitability controls?
rules.Requirement Logic: Brines, sanitizing dips, preservatives, wash solutions and additives must be controlled at defined levels.
rules.Corrective Action: Adjust concentration; hold affected product if limit not met.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Critical if chemical controls safety or legal compliance.
rules.Question_AR: هل process chemical concentrations controlled where used as safety/suitability controls?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Adjust concentration; hold affected product if limit not met.

rules.Question: Are physical contamination controls monitored and verified where used?
rules.Requirement Logic: Detection/sieving controls must have sensitivity/limits, checks, rejects handling and records.
rules.Corrective Action: Stop line; recheck/rework/hold product since last good check.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Failed detection control → product hold/traceability trigger.
rules.Notes: May be CCP in many plants.
rules.Question_AR: هل physical contamination controls monitored and verified where used?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Stop line; recheck/rework/hold product since last good check.

rules.Question: Are heat treatment/cooking lethality parameters defined and monitored for products requiring pathogen reduction?
rules.Requirement Logic: Heat process must define minimum time/temperature or validated equivalent and monitor each batch/lot where relevant.
rules.Corrective Action: Continue process/reprocess only if validated; hold batch until evaluated.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Heat treatment failure is likely CCP deviation.
rules.Notes: Use product-specific limits, not generic assumptions.
rules.Question_AR: هل heat treatment/cooking lethality parameters defined and monitored for products requiring pathogen reduction?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Continue process/reprocess only if validated; hold batch until evaluated.

rules.Question: Is product/core temperature measured at appropriate points and frequency?
rules.Requirement Logic: Monitoring must represent the slowest heating/coldest point or defined critical product point.
rules.Corrective Action: Verify method and reprocess/hold if target not demonstrated.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Insufficient monitoring may invalidate release.
rules.Question_AR: Is product/core temperature measured at appropriate points and frequency?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Verify method and reprocess/hold if target not demonstrated.

rules.Question: After a lethality step, is product protected from recontamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: RTE/cooked product must be protected after kill step by zoning, hygiene, equipment and packaging controls.
rules.Corrective Action: Control exposure; restrict traffic; verify cleaning/personnel; assess affected product if breach.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Post-lethality breach triggers HACCP/M08 review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 zoning; M03 cleaning; M05 hygiene; M08
rules.Question_AR: After a lethality step, is product protected from recontamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Control exposure; restrict traffic; verify cleaning/personnel; assess affected product if breach.

rules.Question: Are cooling parameters defined and monitored where rapid cooling is required?
rules.Requirement Logic: Cooling must prevent growth/toxin formation by meeting validated time/temperature criteria.
rules.Corrective Action: Hold batch if cooling limit missed; evaluate safety; reject/reprocess if unsafe.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Cooling deviation may be CCP/OPRP and product hold trigger.
rules.Question_AR: هل cooling parameters defined and monitored where rapid cooling is required?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Hold batch if cooling limit missed; evaluate safety; reject/reprocess if unsafe.

rules.Question: Is cooling equipment loaded and operated to maintain required cooling rate?
rules.Requirement Logic: Equipment loading, product depth and airflow must support cooling parameters.
rules.Hazard / Risk: Slow cooling due to overload or poor airflow
rules.Corrective Action: Reduce load/depth; improve airflow; validate cooling pattern.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Repeated slow cooling triggers capacity/design review.
rules.Question_AR: Is cooling equipment loaded and operated to maintain required cooling rate?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Reduce load/depth; improve airflow; validate cooling pattern.
rules.HazardRisk_AR: Slow cooling due to overload or poor airflow

rules.Question: Are hot/cold holding temperatures and times controlled during production, storage and service/staging?
rules.Requirement Logic: Holding conditions must prevent microbial growth and maintain product safety.
rules.Corrective Action: Adjust/hold/reject if out of limit; evaluate exposure time.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Holding deviation can trigger product hold/HACCP review.
rules.Question_AR: هل hot/cold holding temperatures and times controlled during production, storage and service/staging?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Adjust/hold/reject if out of limit; evaluate exposure time.

rules.Question: Is thawing controlled to prevent surface temperature abuse, drip contamination and cross-contamination?
rules.Requirement Logic: Thawing must be time/temperature controlled and physically segregated where needed.
rules.Corrective Action: Adjust thawing method; segregate; hold material if abused.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Severe thawing abuse may trigger product hold/HACCP review.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 storage; M03 cleaning; M08 traceability
rules.Question_AR: Is thawing controlled to prevent surface temperature abuse, drip contamination and cross-contamination?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Adjust thawing method; segregate; hold material if abused.

rules.Question: Are raw, partially processed and finished/RTE products separated during operations?
rules.Requirement Logic: Operational practices must prevent raw-to-ready or dirty-to-clean contamination.
rules.Corrective Action: Separate by space/time; clean/changeover; restrict movement; assess exposed product.
rules.HACCP Trigger: Raw-to-RTE operational failure triggers product impact assessment.
rules.Linked Module Impact: M02 layout; M03 cleaning; M05 hygiene; M08
rules.Question_AR: هل raw, partially processed and finished/جاهز للاستهلاك (RTE) products separated during operations?
rules.CorrectiveAction_AR: Separate by space/time; clean/changeover; restrict movement; assess exposed product.

rules.Question: Are utensils, containers, tools and mobile equipment segregated or controlled between risk zones/products?
